com.mentor.chs.plugin.action

Interface IXSymbolAction

  • All Superinterfaces:
    IXBaseAction, IXPlugin
    All Known Subinterfaces:
    IXSymbolApplicationAction

    public interface IXSymbolAction
extends IXBaseAction
    Implement this interface if you need to implement a Custom Action that works on Capital Symbol. Such actions will, therefore, be available in Capital Symbol when a symbol is opened.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      boolean execute(IXSymbolContext symbolContext)
      This is the method that actually does the work for your action.
      boolean isAvailable(IXSymbolContext context)
      Is the symbol Action available now?

      • Methods inherited from interface com.mentor.chs.plugin.action.IXBaseAction

        getLongDescription, getMnemonicKey, getSmallIcon, getTriggers, isReadOnly

      • Methods inherited from interface com.mentor.chs.plugin.IXPlugin

        getDescription, getName, getVersion

    • Method Detail

      • isAvailable

        boolean isAvailable(IXSymbolContext context)
        Is the symbol Action available now?

        This method returns a boolean that indicates whether this plugin is currently available for use by the application.

        The plugin may make this determination based on the available information in the IXSymbolContext passed to it.

        Parameters:
        context - - the application context.
        Returns:
        true, if the plugin is available based on the information in the application context, otherwise false.

      • execute

        boolean execute(IXSymbolContext symbolContext)
        This is the method that actually does the work for your action.

        This is the method that will be called when the custom action's menu entry is clicked. This method must return a Boolean that indicates the success or failure of the custom action. This method need not open a dialog. However, if a dialog is opened, it should be opened modal, for example: 

            boolean execute(IXApplicationContext applicationContext) {
                JDialog dialog = new JDialog(applicationContext.getParentFrame(), true);
      dialog.setTitle(getName());
      //...
      // create your dialog contents here...
      //...
      dialog.setVisible(true);
      return true;
    }

        For compatibility with future releases of Capital, please try to return true or false if your dialog has been OKed or CANCELed, or return false if the operation failed, true if the operation succeeded. If you have made changes to properties/attributes, those changes will NOT be applied unless you return 'true'.

        WARNING: If you have to use frames, NEVER NEVER EVER set the default close option to JFrame.EXIT_ON_CLOSE: 

            JFrame frame = ...
    frame.setDefaultCloseOperation(JFrame.EXIT_ON_CLOSE); // This is BAD
        This will cause the application to exit when you close the frame. This exit will cause the loss of any unsaved data. DO NOT DO THIS. You should use a JDialog or do the following explictly: 
            JFrame frame = ...
    frame.setDefaultCloseOperation(JFrame.DISPOSE_ON_CLOSE); // This is BETTER
        Parameters:
        symbolContext - - the symbol application context. From this object, the currently selected objects can be obtained. Also the current design and/or project.
        Returns:
        should return true, if the action was completed successfully , otherwise false.
